title: Agent identity: migration tooling and backward compatibility
issueBody:
## Summary

Build migration tooling to convert existing AgentStack resources (with inline prompts) into the new AgentPersona + AgentDefinition model. Ensure backward compatibility so existing workflows don't break.

## Scope

1. **Compatibility layer in dispatch controller** - accept `agentDefinition` OR `agentStack`, resolve both paths
2. **Migration script** - extract persona from stack (prompts, skills -> AgentPersona), create binding (AgentDefinition), clean stack
3. **Deprecation warnings** - log warning when dispatch resolves a stack with inline prompts
4. **TriggerRule update** - accept `agentDefinition` field alongside existing `agentStack`
5. **Stack builder UI** - continues creating AgentStack (infra only), separate from persona editor
6. **MCP tool update** - `krate_dispatch_agent` accepts definition or stack ref

## Reference

- [docs/agent-identity/02-migration.md](packages/krate/docs/agent-identity/02-migration.md) - full migration strategy

## Dependencies

- #620 (agent identity core)
issueComments: Triage summary: Migration and compatibility work is needed so Krate can move from inline AgentStack identity data to AgentPersona + AgentDefinition without breaking existing AgentStack workflows. Recommended approach: add/consume #620 identity resources, centralize dispatch target resolution, preserve legacy stack behavior, add prompt composition precedence, update TriggerRule/web dispatch/forms/krate_dispatch_agent metadata, add migration tooling with dry-run default, backup/reversible behavior, safe apply cleanup ordering, and add deprecation warnings.
Existing issue comment notes a prior plan PR #663 and an implementation agent running on that plan. When executing this process, audit the current branch first and implement only remaining gaps.
observedCurrentStateHints: AgentPersona and AgentDefinition resources may already exist from #620 in packages/krate/core/src/resource-model.js and packages/krate/charts/crds/agent-resources.yaml.
Core dispatch may already accept agentDefinition and resolve it through packages/krate/core/src/agent-persona-controller.js.
TriggerRule tests may already cover agentDefinition support.
The web dispatch route packages/krate/web/app/api/orgs/[org]/agents/dispatch/route.js still needs auditing for agentDefinition support.
The Atlas descriptor packages/atlas/graph/extensions/tool-servers/krate-mcp-server.yaml still needs auditing for krate_dispatch_agent accepting definition or stack reference.
No migration script was observed under packages/krate/core/scripts or packages/krate/cli during planning audit.
No deprecation warning path for legacy inline stack prompts/skills was observed during planning audit.
acceptanceCriteria: Legacy AgentStack manual dispatch still works with inline systemPrompt, developerPrompt, taskPrompt, and skillRefs.
Manual dispatch accepts agentDefinition or agentStack and resolves AgentDefinition to persona plus stack before permission review, memory, workspace, context bundle, and job creation.
AgentTriggerRule validation, event evaluation, CRD schema, and tests accept agentDefinition alongside agentStack without requiring both.

Migration tooling defaults to dry-run, extracts inline stack prompts and skillRefs into AgentPersona, creates AgentDefinition binding, emits backup/reversible output, and does not mutate resources unless apply mode is explicit.
Apply mode writes AgentPersona and AgentDefinition successfully before cleaning stack identity fields; partial failures leave the source stack unchanged.
Dispatch emits a deprecation warning when a legacy AgentStack with inline prompt fields or skillRefs is used.
Web dispatch accepts agentDefinition while preserving stackRef and agentStack compatibility.
Stack builder UI continues creating AgentStack infrastructure and does not become the persona editor in this issue.
CLI MCP server and Atlas krate_dispatch_agent descriptor accept definition or stack references.
Docs and examples that describe dispatch or migration contracts are updated only where needed.
Focused tests and broad repo verification pass or document only environment-specific residual risk.
qualityGates: Reuse audit recorded before implementation starts.
Runtime call paths are traced before file edits.
Missing coverage is expressed as tests before production changes.
Migration dry-run and apply behavior are tested for non-destructive defaults and cleanup ordering.
Legacy AgentStack compatibility has explicit regression coverage.
AgentDefinition behavior is covered at core, trigger, web, CLI MCP, and descriptor contract surfaces.
Final review blocks on destructive migration behavior, field contract divergence, or broad unrelated changes.
outOfScope: Full persona editor UI from the separate web experience work.
Removing AgentStack inline prompt support or setting a removal timeline.
Renaming all existing stack references when compatibility can preserve them.
Re-implementing #620 identity core if it already exists on the base branch.
